gaps: MISSING: False success message after DFU failure — No test case validates that the app displays 'updated successfully' (or similar completion message) when the update actually failed. All existing DFU failure tests (65459462, 65459463, 65459464) focus on the 'Houston' error screen with Cancel/Retry, not on the scenario where the app incorrectly reports success.

MISSING: 'Update unavailable' message on retry after failed DFU — No test case covers the scenario where, after a failed DFU, tapping Retry shows 'update unavailable' instead of allowing a retry. This is a distinct failure path not covered by the Houston error flow.

MISSING: State persistence after DFU interruption — The bug involves the app persisting a 'success' state (or corrupted state) after a failed DFU, which then blocks future update attempts. No test validates what firmware version/status is stored and displayed after an interrupted DFU, or how that state affects subsequent retry attempts.

MISSING: App lifecycle interaction with DFU failure state — No test covers what happens when the app is backgrounded/foregrounded or killed/relaunched after a failed DFU that showed false success. The bug's 'update unavailable' on retry may be tied to stale cached state that persists across app restarts.

MISSING: Bluetooth reconnection after DFU failure — The bug involves interrupting Bluetooth during DFU. No test validates the firmware update screen state after the headset reconnects post-failure, specifically whether the app correctly detects the actual (unchanged) firmware version vs. a phantom 'updated' version.

MISSING: Device-specific asymmetry — The bug does not specify which Poly device, but the domain knowledge shows multiple device types (Voyager Surround 80 UC with touch control, Voyager Legend 50/30, Voyager 4210-4220 office system). No existing tests validate DFU failure behavior across different device families, which may have different firmware update protocols and error handling.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FALSE-SUCCESS-001: Verify app does NOT display 'updated successfully' when DFU fails due to Bluetooth disconnection
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the connected device name (e.g., 'Poly Voyager Surround 80 UC')
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Note the current firmware version displayed (e.g., '1.2.3')
Tap 'Check for Update' and confirm an update is available
Tap 'Update Now' to start the firmware update process
During the update progress (at ~30-50%), force a Bluetooth disconnection by turning off the headset or moving out of range
Wait for the app to show a connection lost indicator
Reconnect the headset by powering it on and waiting for Bluetooth reconnection
Observe the Firmware Update screen
generated_testcases.expected: The app does NOT display 'Updated successfully' or any success/completion message. The firmware version shown should still be the original version (e.g., '1.2.3'), not a phantom updated version. The app should display an error or failure state (e.g., 'Update failed', 'Connection lost during update').

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FALSE-SUCCESS-002: Verify app does NOT display 'updated successfully' when DFU fails due to app force-kill during transfer
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the connected device name (e.g., 'Poly Voyager Legend 50')
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Note the current firmware version (e.g., '2.0.1')
Tap 'Check for Update' and confirm an update is available
Tap 'Update Now' to start the firmware update
During the update progress (at ~40-60%), force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps)
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app
Wait for the app to reconnect to the headset automatically
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Observe the firmware version and any status messages
generated_testcases.expected: The app does NOT display 'Updated successfully'. The firmware version should remain the original version (e.g., '2.0.1'). The app should show the update as incomplete/failed, or prompt to retry the update. No false success state is persisted across app restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-RETRY-UNAVAILABLE-001: Verify 'Update unavailable' message when retrying DFU after a failed update that showed false success
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Tap the connected device name (e.g., 'Poly Voyager Surround 80 UC')
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Note the current firmware version (e.g., '3.0.0')
Tap 'Check for Update' and confirm an update is available to version '3.1.0'
Tap 'Update Now' to start the firmware update
During the update (at ~50%), force a Bluetooth disconnection by turning off the headset
Wait 10 seconds, then turn the headset back on
Reconnect to the headset in the app
Observe the Firmware Update screen — note any success/failure message
Tap 'Retry' or 'Check for Update' again
Observe the message displayed
generated_testcases.expected: If the app incorrectly shows 'Updated successfully' (the bug scenario), tapping 'Check for Update' or 'Retry' should NOT show 'Update unavailable'. The app should either allow a retry of the update or correctly show the original firmware version and allow a fresh update attempt. If the bug is present, this test documents the 'Update unavailable' false blocking state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-STATE-PERSISTENCE-001: Verify firmware version displayed after interrupted DFU survives app restart and Bluetooth reconnection
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Connect to a Poly device (e.g., 'Poly Voyager Legend 30')
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Record the current firmware version (e.g., '1.5.0')
Start a firmware update to version '1.6.0'
At ~60% progress, turn off the headset to interrupt the update
Wait 15 seconds
Turn the headset back on and allow Bluetooth reconnection
Observe the Firmware Update screen — record the firmware version shown
Close the Poly Studio Mobile app (background it)
Reopen the app and navigate back to Firmware Update
Record the firmware version shown again
Force-kill the app (swipe away from recent apps)
Reopen the app, reconnect to the headset, navigate to Firmware Update
Record the firmware version shown a third time
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version displayed should consistently show the ORIGINAL version (e.g., '1.5.0') across all three checks — after reconnection, after app background/foreground, and after app force-kill/relaunch. The app should NOT persist a phantom 'updated' version. The version should match what the headset actually reports via Bluetooth.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-BT-RECONNECT-001: Verify firmware update screen correctly reflects actual headset firmware after DFU failure and Bluetooth reconnection cycle
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Connect to a Poly device (e.g., 'Poly Voyager Surround 80 UC')
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Record the current firmware version (e.g., '2.2.0')
Start a firmware update to version '2.3.0'
At ~40% progress, force Bluetooth disconnection by turning off the headset
Wait 5 seconds, turn the headset back on
Allow the app to automatically reconnect to the headset
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Record the firmware version displayed
Disconnect Bluetooth from phone Settings (tap 'Forget this device')
Re-pair the headset with the phone
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and reconnect to the headset
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Record the firmware version displayed a second time
Compare both recorded versions to the original version (2.2.0)
generated_testcases.expected: Both post-reconnection checks should show the ORIGINAL firmware version (2.2.0), not a phantom updated version. The app should correctly read the actual firmware version from the headset after each reconnection cycle, regardless of the interrupted DFU state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-DEVICE-ASYMMETRY-001: Verify DFU failure handling on Poly Voyager Surround 80 UC (touch control device)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Connect to a 'Poly Voyager Surround 80 UC' headset
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Record current firmware version
Start a firmware update
During the update, use the touch control surface on the right earcup: tap to play/pause media
Observe if the touch gesture interrupts the DFU process
If the update continues, swipe to skip track during the update
Observe the app behavior and any error messages
After the update attempt completes (or fails), check the firmware version displayed
Attempt to retry the update by tapping 'Check for Update'
generated_testcases.expected: Touch control gestures during DFU should not corrupt the update state. If the update fails due to touch input interference, the app should show a clear error (not false success). The firmware version should remain unchanged. Retry should be available without showing 'Update unavailable'. The touch control device should handle DFU interruptions gracefully.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-DEVICE-ASYMMETRY-002: Verify DFU failure handling on Poly Voyager 4210-4220 Office System (base station device)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Connect to a 'Poly Voyager 4210/4220' office headset system (headset connected to base)
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Record current firmware version of the headset
Start a firmware update
During the update, lift the headset from the base (simulating a desk phone call)
Observe if the base disconnection interrupts the DFU
Place the headset back on the base
Wait for reconnection
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Record the firmware version displayed
Attempt to retry the update
generated_testcases.expected: The office base station system should handle headset lift/return during DFU without corrupting state. The firmware version should remain the original version. No false success message should appear. The retry option should be available without 'Update unavailable' message. The base station's unique hook-switch behavior should not cause state persistence issues.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-LIFECYCLE-001: Verify DFU failure state after app backgrounding during update and subsequent foregrounding
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app
Connect to a Poly device (e.g., 'Poly Voyager Legend 50')
Navigate to Settings → Firmware Update
Record current firmware version (e.g., '4.0.0')
Start a firmware update to version '4.1.0'
At ~50% progress, press the Home button to background the app
Wait 30 seconds
Reopen the app from the app switcher
Observe the Firmware Update screen
Record the firmware version and any status messages
Tap 'Check for Update' or the available action button
Observe the result
generated_testcases.expected: After backgrounding and foregrounding during an active DFU, the app should not show a false 'Updated successfully' message. The firmware version should remain the original version (4.0.0). The app should either show the update as interrupted/failed or resume the update. No 'Update unavailable' blocking message should appear on retry.
